tldr: Countly is an event-based product and mobile analytics platform, while Baremetrics is focused on SaaS subscription metrics derived from billing data. They cover different domains, so this is a refocus rather than a 1:1 analytics migration.
why_switch: Care more about MRR, churn and SaaS metrics than events
Want a billing-derived metrics dashboard out of the box
Already using a supported billing provider
export_method: From Countly, export events, user profiles and dashboards via the admin UI or API. If you self-host, take a database backup as your authoritative archive — that is the cleanest source of truth for historical analysis. Document the tracking plan separately. Most of this data does not directly map to Baremetrics, so treat the export primarily as long-term storage in your warehouse rather than something to import wholesale.
import_method: Baremetrics ingests data from supported billing providers (such as Stripe or Chargebee) rather than from product analytics tools. Connect your billing source to Baremetrics following its onboarding flow, and let it compute MRR, ARR, churn and LTV directly from subscription data. Use Baremetrics segments and notes to reproduce the high-level views your team relied on in Countly. Keep Countly running for product behavior analysis if you still need it, or replace it separately with another product analytics tool — Baremetrics is not a substitute for event analytics.
effort: 1-3 weeks for Baremetrics setup, longer if Countly use cases must be replaced separately
gotchas: Baremetrics does not replace event analytics — confirm scope before switching
Baremetrics relies on a supported billing provider being in place
Self-hosted Countly history is yours to archive — back it up before shutdown
Mobile-specific Countly metrics have no equivalent in Baremetrics
Definitions of MRR and churn may differ from prior internal calculations
If you still need product analytics, plan a second tool alongside Baremetrics

email.subject: Refocusing analytics from Countly to Baremetrics
email.body:
Hi team,

Over the next 14 days we are connecting Baremetrics to our billing provider and using it as our primary source of SaaS metrics, while we reconsider what we still need from Countly.

The driver is that most current questions are about MRR, churn and LTV, which Baremetrics computes directly from billing data. Countly has been carrying both behavior analytics and revenue context, and we want to split those concerns. Baremetrics will own subscription metrics. Countly will stay live for now for product behavior, and we will decide whether to keep, replace or retire it separately.

During the cutover we will validate Baremetrics' MRR and churn against our internal numbers, agree on definitions, and brief stakeholders on the new dashboards. If you self-host Countly, we will archive its database before any shutdown so historical events are not lost.

If you depend on a Countly dashboard, please flag it this week so we plan around it.

Thanks,
Your customer
